title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jules and Gwendraith plan to travel to Anstey Hall in Dys, Northumberland, to investigate clues regarding Erskine. Jules has written to Kennedy to request Helen’s letters and a handwriting sample. Gwendraith suggests locating Lily Abbott, a perceptive former servant from St. Catherine's Manor who once worked in Torquay, believing she might provide information. Giles agrees to place an advertisement for Lily.

In Dartmouth, Miss Marple visits Mrs. Fenn, a woman she knows through a mutual friend, Eleanor Fern. During their conversation, Mrs. Fenn discusses her three sons: Gerald, who works in Singapore; Robert, who is in the army; and Walter, a homebody who lives at home and works at her firm. Mrs. Fenn reveals that Walter once traveled to India to become a tea planter because he was in love with Helen Kennedy, a woman she considered unsuitable and promiscuous. After Walter went to India, Helen wrote to him claiming she would marry him, but upon arriving at the docks, she rejected him to pursue a married man instead. Mrs. Fenn notes that Walter maintains extreme self-control and buries his emotions deeply. Miss Marple probes into Walter's temperament, leading Mrs. Fenn to recount a childhood incident where Walter, after his brother Robert broke a model airplane he had meticulously built, attacked Robert with a fire stick while screaming that he wanted to kill him.
